subject: plant parents
one_liner: Production-grade machine learning inference for the seventeen plants on your windowsill.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan developer-infra. Talks to a houseplant owner like they are a platform engineer with a burn rate. Every plant gets an endpoint, a version, and an invoice. Confident, technical, never winking. The joke is that nobody asked for GPUs on a pothos and we shipped them anyway, billed per leaf-second.

hero.h1: Deploy machine learning to your windowsill.
hero.sub: Repot is an API-first inference platform for houseplants. Point a webcam at your fern, pick a model, and find out what your fern already knew.

features.title: Versioned plant registry
features.blurb: Every photo of your monstera, tagged and immutable. Cut a release when it sprouts a leaf. Roll back to the version before you overwatered it. The photo is safe. The plant is a separate conversation.

features.title: Sub-five-second cold starts
features.blurb: Your is-this-basil-dead endpoint sits at zero until you panic at 11pm. It spins up in 4.2 seconds, returns a verdict, and scales back down. Billed per leaf-second, rounded up.

features.title: Autoscaling foliage
features.blurb: Buy a fourth pothos and we provision a fourth GPU. The plant does not know it has a GPU. The GPU does not know it has a plant. The invoice has been fully briefed.

pricing_tiers.name: Hobby
perks: Shared GPU, when one is free
No SLA on whether the plant is alive

pricing_tiers.name: Greenhouse
perks: One GPU pinned to each plant

pricing_tiers.name: Arboretum
pricing_tiers.tagline: For people who call their apartment a collection.
perks: A solutions engineer who has also killed a fiddle-leaf fig

testimonials.quote: I deployed a model to tell me the pothos needed water. It told me what I already knew, with 94% confidence. I have never felt so seen and so audited at the same time.
testimonials.name: Dana Reyes
testimonials.title: Senior Plant Parent, two monsteras and one fiddle-leaf fig in hospice

testimonials.quote: We migrated the entire succulent shelf to Repot over a weekend. Three of them have since died, but the dashboards are immaculate and the latency is excellent.
testimonials.name: Marcus Liu
testimonials.title: Head of Foliage Reliability, home office, no reports

testimonials.quote: The cold starts are faster than my last three therapists. I do not know what the model does. I run it every morning before coffee.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand
testimonials.title: Individual Contributor on a single snake plant

demo_screen_concept: An inference console for one houseplant, modeled on a model-run page. Left rail: the user's plants as a selectable list (Monstera "Greg", Pothos "The Survivor", Basil "Probationary"), each with a live status dot and uptime. Center: a webcam still of the selected plant, a model picker (leaf-wilt-detector v3, soil-moisture-vibes v2, will-it-survive-the-weekend, is-this-a-bug-or-dirt), and a large Run inference button. On run: a fake latency readout, a streaming JSON output panel with predictions and confidence scores typed out line by line, and a confidence gauge whose needle animates to the result. Right rail: a billing meter ticking up in leaf-seconds and a version-history list. Interactive: switch plants, pick models, run inference (mock client-side streaming), watch the meter climb.

design_direction.style: Developer-platform technical docs, light. Cool near-white paper ground, hairline gray-green rules, generous whitespace and a precise gridded feel. Geist Mono for all metadata, labels, metrics and tabular numerals; Geist for headings and body. A single infra-green accent does all the work as a status/run color (deploy buttons, live dots, the confidence gauge, code-fence chips). Inline product nouns are styled like inline code. No gradients, no glow, no rounded blobs. Reads like the documentation of a deadly-serious inference platform that happens to host pothos.
design_direction.archetype: Developer platform / API product landing page
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained, 150-250ms ease-out. Hairline underline reveals on links, a deploy button that transitions through a brief spinner to a 'deployed' state, mono counters that tick, a gauge needle that eases to position. All gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left-aligned hero: a mono eyebrow ('v3.2 / inference platform'), a large Geist headline, Geist subhead, two CTAs (solid green 'Deploy a plant', ghost 'Read the docs'). Right side is a hairline-bordered console card showing an example API call against a plant (curl POST to /v1/plants/greg/infer) with a JSON response in IBM Plex Mono. A thin status strip below the fold reads 'all systems operational / 1 plant alive'.
